Brother idk what people are yapping about but this is garbage. As someone who loves the musical, i was excited there was a movie coming out for this. And then I've been served with this. First of all. They butchered all the songs. Ariana grande needs to be stopped. Second, the cgi is garbage. Third, why the heck is there a fighting montage? For some reason Hollywood makes it so that in every movie with a school has to have some sort of fighting class. Finally, this just looks like a cash grab. The only good part is when Galenda says she doesn't want to give away the hat because she doesn't hate anyone that much, and then gives Elphaba the hat. Overall, i don't think to much heart was put into it , and it needs a new soundtrack. Also why haven't they mentioned that there's going to be 2 parts? Super dumb. I liked the set though.

This is the actual story from the book. The play was loosely based off of. This should be able to bring this great novel to many more people. The book had a little bit of everything in this prequel story that led up to the Wizard of Oz! Love, sex, betrayal, politics, friendship, advocacy, power, government and control. I always enjoyed the musical but was bummed it only scratched the surface of the story that unfolded in the novel. So grateful they are finally bringing this novel to life!❤🎉❤🎉
So glad to see this wonderful book and story to finally make it to the big screen!❤🎉 and with the perfect director and cool cast.

Oh, gosh! I SERIOUSLY hope the movie is good! I've gone to see the Broadway performance twice (once for a school trip and the second for my birthday), but something went wrong both times that ruined my experience.

The first time, a fire had broken out backstage during the song "No Good Deed" and we all had to wait outside in the cold as they tried to get a handle the situation. They did eventually put out the fire and were able to continue the performance, but by then our school trip had to come to an end and we had to leave before we could see the rest of the show. What really sucked is that, by the time we were finally allowed back inside, we were told it was time to leave. That REALLY sucked!

The second time, my dad had gotten tickets for us to see the show for my birthday and I was really excited! I had wanted to see the rest of the play ever since my last attempt to watch it was cut short because of the fire and tight schedule to get us home, so I was thrilled when Dad surprised me with tickets to the next time Wicked would be in town. Unfortunately, there was a mix up with the tickets my dad got that were meant for a late night performance after we had already arrived. He was able to exchange them for tickets for the right time, but the seats were HORRIBLE. We were seated in a really unfortunate spot due to a railing blocking my view of most of the performance. I'm on the short side compared to the rest of my family and so I a really difficult time seeing over the railing that kept blocking my view. I even tried switching seats with my Dad and I still couldn't see the show. I still remember how upsetting it was that the rail blocked the parts of the stage where most of the action took place and so I missed a lot of what was going on despite my best efforts. I couldn't enjoy myself because I couldn't see anything because of that stupid railing!

I was so disappointed that I had gone to see Wicked twice but both times something went wrong that ruined my experience. Maybe it's true that the third time is the charm, but I'm a little afraid that something will go wrong again and I'll be let down that I miss out on being able to properly watch this masterpiece again. At this point, I'm honestly concerned that someone might have cursed me to never be able to truly enjoy Wicked as I wish.

However, with this announcement of a Wicked movie, I'm genuinely hopefully that I'll FINALLY get to see it without any issues! I really hope it lives up to the stage performance! I'm really looking forward to this movie! The trailer looks so good and has me HYPED!

I’m not a fan of the choice of actor for Elphaba. Becasue  I saw the broadway show and read the book …what I see here anyway doesn’t match the character. She doesn’t start for wearing black. She wears gray and hides her face. In the play her being different wasn’t mainly about her skin color it was about her thinking for herself and recognizing what was happening was wrong. She realized the wizard was making animals (some of who were intelligent and could speak and work) revert back to being “true” animals. The voice of Elphaba doesn’t match either. I know this is just a small trailer but 2 characters I’ve related to have been recast as someone with a darker skin tone. I wish they hadn’t done that because in this story it wasn’t about race. There were munchkins after all and intelligent animals who could speak wear clothes and teach in a university. It was about her mind and her will to think for herself and not be swayed (even by magic)

With the movie, I'm seeing potential for an actual sequel to the story. We know from the stage production that Elphaba fled Oz with Fyero, and Morrible went to prison. Morrible is clever enough to talk or threaten her way out so she could take revenge on Glinda and rule Oz herself. With no real powers, Glinda would find herself at Morrible's mercy, and wishing her BFF were still alive to help. Meanwhile there is one character in Oz who likely knows Elphaba is alive, and maybe even where she went - the monkey who handed Elphaba the note prior to the "For Good" number. What if he read the note, figures out where to find Elphaba (perhaps living in Kansas?) and fetches her back to rescue Glinda one more time. Perhaps bringing Fyero & Dorothy along too...Just wishful thinking.

Okay. I don't want to make any pre-screening judgements but there are definitely some things I would've done differently, like I would've made it all one movie with an intermission for theatrical release only. I think Cynthia Erivo is super talented, but I don't know if she's the right one for Elphaba. I think she should've done "The Color Purple" instead of Fantasia, and I know Idina Menzel is 52 but I still would've had her as Elphaba with makeup and visual effects de-aging her like Sigourney Weaver in "Avatar: The Way of Water". I feel the same way about Ariana Grande, Jonathan Bailey and Michelle Yeoh, I would've done Amanda Seyfried as Glinda, Aaron Tveit as Fiyero and Meryl Streep as Madame Morrible. I also would've included Kristen Chenoweth and Joel Grey in the cast. Everyone can disagree but be respectful to me please, I'm just stating my opinion. We will just have to wait and see if these choices were right, if my views change after seeing it I will let everybody know.

I first knew about this when I was in highschool and dreamed of being part of our local school play for senior year (kinda mandatory for seniors to produce a play in our school). Our class was eventually given "Mama Mia" instead though. But since my freshman year, I have memorized every track especially Galinda's lines (despite me being disgusted by pink at that time) simply because I was glad I found a character that finally has a ridiculously high pitched talking voice--same kind of voice I was bullied my whole life with, even now as an adult. I wasn't good at singing anyways so my dream of at least becoming an extra in our Wicked play would have been farfetched even if we were assigned to produce Wicked. 😅 Still, it always has a special place in my heart, even if I never saw the play in person even once (I don't live in the US).
It has been 19 years since then--and now it finally has a movie.

Sooo awesome! #2 on Trending, even more awesome! Cynthia is very likely to become a favorite amongst the long line of Elphies, and Ariana is surprisingly great at Galinda. She definitely giving Chenoweth vibes, and if she plans on modeling herself after Kristin's performance, then I applaud her for going the OG route. Also, not sure how many Galindas in the stage show have given the character a southern accent but it totally works. Oz is looking great, Michelle and Jeff are great, and I like that Doctor Dillamond was featured. That being said, there doesn't seem to be that much focus on Nessa and Boq. Not too concerned yet, because at least we got a brief glimpse of Nessa. My only nitpick is that there seems to be a bit too much synthesizer during Popular (not during the "las" which sound beautiful), but it still sounds great. All in all, Thanksgiving can't come soon enough! Some fans have been waiting 21 years for this to happen, and finally in six months it will finally arrive!

So, I don't actually know the musical (sorry) and this movie looks pretty spectacular visually, but I'm so sick of all these different stories that make the villains into heroes.  Like, Wicked Witch is NOT a hero, she is EVIL.  The Wizard is NOT evil, he is GOOD.  These inversions aren't creative, they're essentially revisionist fiction.

All that having been said, I think Wicked was one of the first to do this trope, so I'm likely to forgive it and see it anyway, but man I'm sick of it.
